mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in 60% of periods following a federal funds rate decline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in 70% of instances when unemployment crosses above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts confirm collapsing consumer demand, forcing earnings downgrades and producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent annualized during high inflation periods demonstrates supply-side resilience that offsets price pressures, supporting positive medium-term equity returns.
failure_condition: High industrial production growth coincides with negative 60-day returns during periods when CPI exceeds 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a stagnant federal funds rate compresses real interest rates, increasing the relative attractiveness of equities and driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Real rate compression fails to generate positive 20-day returns when inflation rises faster than policy rates.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand the labor force and aggregate demand, directly boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently coincides with negative or flat 20-day forward returns across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annualized confirms economic contraction, triggering immediate portfolio de-risking and negative short-term market repricing.
failure_condition: S&P 500 remains flat or positive in 65% of quarters where annualized GDP growth drops below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while total employment falls indicates productivity-driven supply expansion, lowering unit costs and supporting positive medium-term equity valuations.
failure_condition: Productivity divergence yields negative 60-day returns in the majority of observed instances.
